name: Apple Pancakes from the Townships
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Apples; unpeeled, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, sift together flour, 2 tablespoons baking powder, 2 tablespoons baking soda, salt, sugar, and cinnamon.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs and buttermilk until well combined.
3. Add the apple pieces and melted butter to the egg mixture and stir until just combined.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, stirring until smooth.
5. Heat a greased cast iron skillet over medium heat.
6. Pour about 1/4 cup batter per pancake onto the hot skillet.
7. Cook pancakes until golden brown on both sides.
8. Serve plain or with butter and maple syrup.
Each year at the end of September, the whole family would go to the Eastern Townships Apple Festival. And there, instead of eggs in syrup as was the custom at sugaring parties, apples pancakes were served with lots of butter and delicious Townships maple syrup.
